expectations.text: Explains that a single rename is unsafe during rolling deploys because old code writes to the old column name
expectations.evidence: Old and new code run simultaneously; 'Old code keeps writing to email. After the rename, that column no longer exists - those writes error out', plus AR caches column list causing crashes.

expectations.text: Provides a multi-deploy sequence with at least 3 distinct steps/deploys
expectations.evidence: Explicit 3-deploy expand/contract: Deploy 1 Expand, Deploy 2 Switch, Deploy 3 Contract, plus intermediate backfill/constraint/index steps with summary table.

expectations.text: Shows migration code for adding the new column
expectations.evidence: AddEmailAddressToUsers with add_column :users, :email_address, :string shown with full migration class.

expectations.text: Shows backfill migration using disable_ddl_transaction! and in_batches
expectations.evidence: BackfillEmailAddress declares disable_ddl_transaction! and uses User.unscoped.in_batches(of: 10_000) with batched update_all.

expectations.text: Mentions ignored_columns before dropping the old column
expectations.evidence: Deploy 2 sets self.ignored_columns += ["email"] with explanation that it makes dropping safe, before Deploy 3 remove_column.

expectations.text: Shows migration code for dropping the old column using safety_assured

eval_feedback.overall: Fully satisfies all six expectations with correct migration code and a clear 3-deploy expand/contract narrative. Bonus: concurrent index, NOT NULL restoration via NOT VALID check constraint, strong_migrations setup.
